Chicago Safety & Commute Atlas · Krasan Consulting Relocation

One map for the whole question: where's safe, and how do you get to work?

475,651 reported incidents (June 2024-June 2026), mapped to the beat - with live incident icons, hotspots, nearby transit, and an address search that compares your transit vs driving commute to the Loop office.

475,651 incidents
Jun 2024-Jun 2026
22 districts · 50 wards · 274 beats
Source: Chicago Data Portal ijzp-q8t2
01

The Interactive Atlas

One map, all layers. Shade districts/wards/beats by crime, drop incident icons or a heatmap, show nearby transit, and type a home address to see its crime surroundings and a side-by-side transit-vs-driving commute to Krasan.

Try it Type a home address in the panel (top right) to see its ½-mile crime count, nearest CTA/Metra station, and a live transit-vs-driving commute to the Loop office.

Map: Leaflet/CARTO · transit: OpenStreetMap · driving: OSRM · transit time: Google Maps. Needs internet.
Q1 / Q2 - lowest crime per month?
Color by All crime: District 20 (Edgewater) is lowest at ~420/mo. Color by Violent crime: District 20 again leads at ~129/mo, then Logan Square, Albany Park, Jefferson Park. The safe cluster sits on the North and far Southwest edges.
Q8 - does living closer to the Loop raise violent exposure?
Yes. The Loop (D1) runs ~295 violent/mo - more than 2× Edgewater's 129. Search a downtown address and check the ½-mile crime count vs a North-side one.
02

The Headline

Rank on violent crime, not raw totals. Here's where that lands.

D20
Edgewater is the safest district - lowest TOTAL and VIOLENT crime of all 22
2×
More violent crime in the Loop (downtown) than Edgewater - ~295 vs 129 a month
44%
of Austin's crime is violent - vs ~35% citywide. Low total, high violence

Violent crime per month, by district

Lower = safer · ranks all 22 districts by violent crime (Q2)
Note: ranked by raw incident counts; a true per-resident rate would need district population, which isn't in this dataset. Total-crime volume lives on the map ("All crime" shading) and the scatter's x-axis below.
03

Is Total Crime a Reliable Safety Measure?

Not on its own. Some low-volume districts hide high violence - so we rank on violent crime.

Crime volume vs. violent-crime rate

Each dot = a district (numbered), split at the median into four zones
The top-left zone - low volume but high violent share (Austin, 15) - is why total crime alone misleads. Quadrants split at the median; the lines are a guide, not a hard cutoff.
Q3 - is total count reliable?
Partly. District 15 (Austin) sits below the median on total crime, yet 44% of its incidents are violent - far above the safe districts. Volume alone would mislabel it safe.
Mid-analysis - low on both counts?
The genuinely low-on-both districts are 20, 17, 14, 16, 22, 24. Austin clears a loose median bar but stays violent - see the table in section 07.
04

When Is Risk Lowest?

Crime by hour of day

Share of incidents · green = safest (2-7 AM) · amber = busy daytime peak · the midnight bar is mostly unknown-time records, not a real surge

Crime by day of week

Total incidents
Q4 / Q9 - time of day and day of week
Crime is lowest overnight, roughly 2-7 AM (bottoming near 5 AM), then climbs through the day to a midday-and-evening peak (a noon spike, then 3-7 PM). The big bar at midnight is partly an artifact - incidents with unknown times default to 00:00. Day of week barely moves (~1-point spread, Friday highest), so it's too weak to influence the recommendation.
05

What Are the Incidents?

Most common crime types

Share of all incidents · top 10
Q5 - most common types
Theft (23%) and battery (18%) dominate, then criminal damage, assault, and motor-vehicle theft. The most serious violent categories are rare - which is why ranking on violent crime sharpens the safety read.
06

Where to Live - Safety Meets Commute

All in the safest districts, all with a one-seat ride to 111 W Jackson Blvd. Use the map's address search to compare transit vs driving for any specific listing.

Q6 / Q7 - safe + reasonable commute? Transit vs long haul?
No hard tradeoff. Edgewater (Red Line, ~35 min) is the best all-around. Beverly wins on commute via Metra Rock Island to LaSalle St (a block from HQ). Driving is often slower than transit at rush hour and adds parking cost - the map's address tool shows both for any address.
07

Low on Both Counts

Austin (D15) is the cautionary tale - low total, high violence. Which districts are genuinely low on both?

DistrictAreaTotal/moViolent/moVerdict
Krasan Consulting · 111 W Jackson Blvd, Suite 1700, Chicago, IL 60604.
City of Chicago "Crimes - 2001 to Present" (ijzp-q8t2), June 2024-June 2026. Violent = arson, assault, battery, criminal sexual assault, homicide, human trafficking, intimidation, kidnapping, offenses involving children, robbery, sex offense, stalking, weapons violation. Police district & beat boundaries are official; ward outlines derived from incident points. Map for analysis, not navigation.
